topic.what: Distinguishability between multiplication and squaring in ECC hardware causing side-channel leakage of scalar k
topic.how: Power analysis exploiting 1-cycle address-bit differences in multiplexer switching patterns when processing identical vs different operands
topic.why_matters: Reveals secret scalar k in ECC kP operations even when atomic patterns obscure operation-level content

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to distinguish k bit values from power traces
characteristics.evidence: Power trace comparison shows distinguishable red-line activity for multiplication (different registers) vs squaring (same register)
characteristics.context: On IHP 250nm SGB25V library at 30ns clock cycle for NIST P-256 using Longa atomic patterns

characteristics.evidence: Bus reloading requires extra cycle per multiplication/squaring; dummy register method does not affect timing
characteristics.context: For kP requiring many multiplications/squarings; data redirection adds 1 cycle per field operation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Energy difference in 2nd clock cycle due to address switching
characteristics.evidence: Multiplexer activity produces red-line consumption for multiplication but flat line for squaring in 2nd cycle
characteristics.context: 1-cycle window at controller address selection stage

characteristics.what_it_measures: Silicon area increase from dummy register
characteristics.evidence: Dummy register method increases design area and costs
characteristics.context: Single dummy register addition to prevent address-based leakage

characteristics.evidence: Dummy register slightly raises energy; bus-reload increases energy per operation
characteristics.context: Trade-off between leakage prevention and power overhead

novelty: Demonstrates atomic-pattern vulnerability to multiplication-squaring address-bit distinguishability
Quantifies 1-cycle multiplexer leakage as root cause
Compares execution-time vs area tradeoffs for countermeasures
open_problems: Field addition operations may exhibit similar address-bit leakage
Optimal multiplexer design to minimize switching gate count
Extension to other atomic pattern schemes beyond Longa
Scalability to larger operand widths and curve parameters
